entries.title: The Ghetto Sound of Lisbon (2014)
entries.description: For the last ten years, a thrilling form of African-influenced dance music has been brewing in bedrooms in Lisbon's heavily isolated housing projects. But through the pioneering work of a label called Príncipe, the sound is beginning to reach far beyond its local roots.

entries.title: Moodymann: The Spirit of Detroit (2023)
entries.description: "I like to keep it Black and strong around here." Three decades into his career, the effortlessly cool artist is still repping the D and its people whenever he can. He granted us rare access into his world to unpack his love of music and the community that made him.

entries.title: Sonideros: The Mobile Soundsystems of Mexico City (2018)
entries.description: Max Pearl reports from the Mexican capital on a timeless DJ tradition that stretches back to the 1950s.

entries.title: Nyege Nyege: East Africa's New Wave (2017)
entries.description: Electronic music is booming in East Africa, and a small collective based in Kampala is at the heart of it all. Aaron Coultate travelled to Uganda to hear their story.

entries.title: Chasing The Perfect Loop (2020)
entries.description: Why is ultra-repetitive music so special? Matt Unicomb speaks with Robert Hood, William Basinski and more to find out.

entries.title: The Best Releases of 2025
entries.description: RA staff pick their favourite tracks, records and mixes of the past 12 months, broken down into six musical themes.

entries.title: The Best Electronic Tracks of 2000-25
entries.description: Alongside Records and Mixes, here are RA's favourite 550 Tracks of the 21st century so far.

entries.title: The Best Electronic Records of 2000-25
entries.description: Alongside Tracks and Mixes, here are RA's favourite Albums, EPs, Compilations and Club Releases of the 21st century so far.

entries.title: The Best Electronic Mixes of 2000-25
entries.description: Alongside Records and Tracks, here are RA's favourite DJ Mixes, Radio Shows and Live Sets of the 21st century so far.

entries.title: 22 Releases Our Staff Couldn't Stop Playing This Winter
entries.description: Ten staff members select their favourite albums, singles and mixes of recent months. Any genre goes.

entries.title: A History of 2020s Prog in Ten Tracks
entries.description: Roza Terenzi and Kalahari Oyster Cult boss Rey Colino trace the birth of the sub-genre that recoded '90s trance, acid and more into a modern rave moment.

entries.title: Art in the Age of Intimidation
entries.description: From EXIT Festival to Berlin's fallout over Gaza, political pressure has imperilled state funding for the arts. With the US on the brink, Chloe Lula asks: is this the end of cultural soft power?

entries.title: The Art of DJing: KI/KI
entries.description: How do you move 40,000 people? Few have ascended to arena level faster than the Dutch trance star. She spoke about scale, spectacle and the mindset required for success.

entries.title: The Hidden Histories of America's Black Queer Clubs
entries.description: In the '80s and '90s, these spaces birthed dance icons, genres and subcultures. Why do so few remain? madison moore charts their decline and lifelong impact.

entries.title: The Big Business of Pop-Up Raves
entries.description: From disruptive parties to stunts at Ministry of Sound, the viral formula of UK promoter Lab54 proves spectacle sells for a new generation. But at what cost? Ed Gillett investigates.

entries.title: The Best Club Songs Named After Clubs
entries.description: From Berghain and Red Rocks to Liquid Room and Cxema, we compiled a list of musical odes to dance floors around the world.

entries.title: A History of Early Electroclash in Ten Tracks
entries.description: DJ Hell and The Hacker pick electroclash's key anthems between 1997 and 2001, revisiting the blueprints for the rule-breaking sub-genre still rocking dance floors 30 years later.

entries.title: Corsica Studios: Nothing Lasts Forever
entries.description: After 24 years, one of London's great clubs is calling time. 35 participants tell the story in this bumper oral history.

entries.title: The Shock End of Savage Hanoi
entries.description: On New Year's Eve, the treasured Vietnamese club shut prematurely. Vivian Yeung examines its impact and the trend of closures across East and Southeast Asia.

entries.title: Honey Dijon: Pleasure Magnet
entries.description: DJ, producer, fashion muse. In our spring 2026 cover story, Shaad D'Souza asks: what does Honey Dijon truly want?

entries.title: Top Ten Festivals 2026: June
entries.description: Each month, we highlight ten of the best festivals happening around the world.

entries.title: Best Music 2026: April
entries.description: The best albums, EPs and mixes of January, February, March and April 2026.

entries.title: Klymax Discotheque: Bali's Temple of Sound
entries.description: Beyond Bali's beaches lies one of the best audiophile clubs in the world. Guided by co-creator and resident DJ Harvey, its bespoke quad-stack system renders music in ultra-high definition. Welcome to paradise.

entries.title: Respect the Architects: Movement 2026
entries.description: Resident Advisor, Detroit Techno Foundation & Underground Music Academy present an exhibition exploring some of the Detroit electronic music community's most impactful heroes.

entries.title: Electronic Music's Most Memorable Launches
entries.description: Blimps, DJ booking stunts, art installation disasters: we look back at some of the most bizarre and unexpectedly influential rollouts.

entries.title: New 7,000-capacity warehouse venue to open in East London
entries.description: Located in the Royal Docks, Ironworks will launch this October.

entries.title: Legendary Manchester club The White Hotel to close
entries.description: The news was confirmed by the Guardian today.

entries.title: The White House uses new Boards of Canada track for promo video
entries.description: "Deep Time" was used as the soundtrack to a 15-second clip the Trump administration shared to X today.

entries.title: Daft Punk's Thomas Bangalter shares new remix for CHANEL campaign
entries.description: The original version of "Circonvolutions" first featured on his 2023 orchestral album Mythologies.

entries.title: Jeff Mills' new album is set in the year 2097
entries.description: The Trip To Vega is out on June 19th.

entries.title: Heading to Barcelona in June? Here's a guide to the best parties
entries.description: John Talabot, Josey Rebelle, DMX Krew and Polygonia are among the hundreds of artists headed for Catalonia next month.

entries.title: 4AD unveils Cocteau Twins-inspired football shirt ahead of FIFA World Cup
entries.description: The Complicated Opposition capsule also drew inspiration from Scott Walker, Colourbox and Air Miami.

entries.title: Nina Protocol to shut down
entries.description: The blockchain-based marketplace and streaming site has been "unable to find a revenue strategy" to sustain the business.

entries.title: This week's new music: Ikonika, Kaitlyn Aurelia Smith, Speaker Music
entries.description: We round up ten new music announcements from the last few days.

entries.title: Juno Download shuts down after more than two decades
entries.description: The long-running digital music store has ceased operations, with its Instagram account also appearing to have been removed.

entries.title: Last.fm returns to independence after 19 years under corporate ownership
entries.description: The music discovery and listening-tracking platform has split from Paramount Skydance.

entries.title: FISHER to play garden gnome in Toy Story 5
entries.description: The Australian DJ will voice a new character in the local release of the Disney and Pixar franchise.

entries.title: Corfu's PHAEX Festival reveals full lineup for fifth edition
entries.description: Ben Klock, ALIGNMENT and AEREA will all play the multi-venue event this summer.

entries.title: Manuel Fogliata's final recordings released one year after his death
entries.description: Moments comprises four long-form ambient works created during the final period of the artist formerly known as Nuel's life.

entries.title: Charli XCX announces new album Music, Fashion, Film
entries.description: The follow-up to Brat lands on July 24th and features recent singles SS26 and Rock Music.

entries.title: M.I.A. sues Kid Cudi for $2.8 million over Rebel Ragers tour dismissal
entries.description: The London-born artist alleges she was removed from the US tour to generate publicity and boost ticket sales after comments made during her opening set.

entries.title: New York's Agapē Festival reveals 2026 lineup
entries.description: The inaugural edition of the two-day event will take place at Industry City in Brooklyn this September.

entries.title: French festival La Louverie reveals 2026 lineup
entries.description: The five-day event returns to Loir-et-Cher this August.

entries.title: Björk opens major exhibition at the National Gallery of Iceland
entries.description: The Reykjavík show features new and reworked audiovisual installations, while a solar eclipse rave is set for August.

entries.title: London promoter Art of Dark celebrates 15 years with 21-hour party
entries.description: The celebrations will take place across FOLD and Colour Factory next month.

entries.title: UNIIQU3 announces debut album, shares lead single
entries.description: Mile High Club is out now.

entries.title: New audiophile bar and café Slomo opens in Hamburg
entries.description: The Japanese kissaten-inspired space opened in the HafenCity district last month.

entries.title: Model/Actriz, Moor Mother, DJ Haram to play Lebanon fundraiser stream
entries.description: The 12-hour online event will be hosted by Yaraka Radio on June 8th.

entries.title: San Francisco nightlife pioneer Ira Sandler dies
entries.description: The founder of local institution 1015 Folsom was 73 years old.

entries.title: Australian festivals Overtone, Freeform, Mode reveal 2026 lineups
entries.description: The Bizarro-run events will take place across in Kombumerri, Naarm and Eora on the same October weekend.

entries.title: Sydney venue Mary's Underground to close after seven years
entries.description: In a statement, the team cited financial circumstances for the closure.

entries.title: Mix Of The Day: Cascou
entries.description: Acid folk, warm Balearic and soft-hued jazz-funk.

entries.title: Amsterdam's Club RAUM announces new room, Nexus, opening this September
entries.description: The new space will debut during the SPIELRAUM Weekender.

entries.title: Dexter Wansel, Philadelphia sound pioneer, dies aged 75
entries.description: "The space man finally made it to Mars," his son shared on Instagram.

entries.title: Jail Time Records documentary to premiere at Tribeca Film Festival
entries.description: The 94-minute film is shot in Cameroon's New Bell Prison, home to the continent's first prison recording studio.

entries.title: New book chronicles oral history of dubstep
entries.description: Lauren Martin's Aftershock: The Seismic Impact of Dubstep features interviews with 28 key figures from the scene.

entries.title: Rome venue THE MAGICK BAR unveils programme for new season
entries.description: The open-air venue has booked Donato Dozzy, Vlada, Kia and Efdemin for the coming weeks.

entries.title: RA.1036 Tony Humphries
entries.description: A rare snapshot of house music's early days, captured through an unheard 1986 broadcast from the East Coast pioneer.

entries.title: RA.1037 Lola Haro
entries.description: 80 minutes of spectral techno, electro and leftfield obscurities from an emergent star of the Belgian underground.

entries.title: RA.1038 The Trip
entries.description: 90 minutes of blissful, sun-soaked house from the essential UK producer duo.

entries.title: RA.1039 K Wata
entries.description: Sleek, sensual bass science that sketches a new blueprint for dub in the 2020s.

entries.title: RA.1040 Clementaum
entries.description: The São Paulo-based artist tears through baile funk, ballroom and techno in a turbo-charged showcase of global club music's cutting edge.

entries.title: RA.1041 Chlär
entries.description: Undeniably funky techno.

entries.title: Various Artists - Djax-Up-Beats 1990-2005: Volume 1 - The Acid Trip
entries.description: The sound of '90s acid at full throttle, courtesy of one of its key architects.

entries.title: John Summit - CTRL ESCAPE
entries.description: Leading frat house's new wave, the US star serves up a second album packed with cheap thrills and little payoff.

entries.title: Jump Source - Fold
entries.description: Priori and Patrick Holland have been on a tear, but their debut LP as Jump Source hits harder. It's one of the sharpest, best-built electronic albums you'll hear this year.

entries.title: Loukeman - SD-3
entries.description: A restless collage of folk, pop, R&B and rap, stitched into emotionally charged electronics.

entries.title: james K - Friend Remixes
entries.description: One of last year's best albums is recast into a set of aqueous club jams, anchored throughout by the precision of james K's voice.

entries.title: Thaiboy Digital & swedm® - Paradise
entries.description: The Drain Gang mainstay's latest LP takes on big-room trance with aplomb.

entries.title: Boards of Canada - Inferno
entries.description: Warp's IDM icons resurface after 13 years with a dark, disquieting record shaped by a violent world. Say goodbye to the fuzz and wistfulness.

entries.title: fakemink - Terrified
entries.description: After crossing over on a wave of hype, the self-proclaimed saviour of the UK underground wastes his moment on a bloated album of trap, self-aggrandisement and poorly mixed electronics.

entries.title: Jasmín - Bite The Hand That Feeds You
entries.description: Hessle Audio's 50th release reflects on the label's techno-dubstep roots while barrelling bass-first into the future.

entries.title: Beatrice M. - Midnight Swim
entries.description: Another hit from France's best hope for a modern day dubstep great.

entries.title: Mala & Magugu - Militant Don
entries.description: Mala goes back to basics with heavyweight results.

entries.title: Excalibur and DJ Spence - Medium Rare
entries.description: Fatigued by the fast pace of algorithmic slop? Reach for this EP of unhurried perfection from one of minimal's best labels.

entries.title: Zora Jones - ANGEL CRISIS
entries.description: Zora Jones's first solo project in five years ushers in a new era with transformative, high-drama bass music.

entries.title: RHR - GÍRIA
entries.description: Thrilling baile funk fusions from one of São Paulo's most compelling club whisperers.

entries.title: Boards of Canada - Tape 05
entries.description: Warp's mysterious duo are back after 13 years. The surprise: it's some of their most beautiful work yet.

entries.title: Reptant - Ballet Robotique
entries.description: Ice-cold 808s from electro's new prince.

entries.title: Playlist: Horst Arts & Music 2025
entries.description: Listen to 85 tracks that defined this year's edition of the Brussels festival.

entries.title: Playlist: Houghton 2025
entries.description: Listen to 110 tracks RA staff heard on the ground at the UK festival this year.

entries.title: Playlist: Draaimolen 2025
entries.description: Listen to 66 tracks heard by RA staff at the Dutch festival last weekend.

entries.title: Playlist: Horst 2026
entries.description: Listen to 65 tracks that the RA team heard on the ground at this year's festival.

entries.description: Spacey techno, breaks and bass.

entries.description: An all-productions mix of new, original and unreleased tunes.

entries.title: Mix Of The Day: ophélie
entries.description: A dubby closing set from one of Berlin's rising stars.

entries.title: Mix Of The Day: Lefto Early Bird

entries.title: Mix Of The Day: Karl Kobs

entries.title: Mix Of The Day: Plus One

entries.title: Mix Of The Day: Hyas & Tactic
entries.description: Divine house and jazzy broken beat.

entries.title: Mix Of The Day: Icehead

entries.title: Mix Of The Day: Diogo Lacerda & Godero
entries.description: Deep cuts on one of Lisbon's best sound systems.

entries.title: Mix Of The Day: Moonshine
entries.description: A global club music showcase direct from a Zanzibari beach.

entries.title: Mix Of The Day: Sisi
entries.description: Remembering a legend of Japanese dance music.

entries.title: Mix Of The Day: Pépe

entries.title: Mix Of The Day: Larry Heard
entries.description: An exclusive recording of the master in action from 2005.

entries.title: Mix Of The Day: Ciel
entries.description: Chillout music with an aqueous feel.

entries.title: Mix Of The Day: Patrick Russell
entries.description: One of New York's sharpest selectors outdoes himself.

entries.title: Mix Of The Day: Bibi
entries.description: Effortlessly engrossing selections.

entries.title: Mix Of The Day: EVEHIVE
entries.description: Baile funk, techno and club hybrids recorded live from Carnaval.

entries.title: Mix Of The Day: oma totem
entries.description: An absorbing live set from the multifaceted Catalan artist.

entries.description: Dubwise delights from the beloved Athenian DJs.

entries.description: Buttery smooth Afro tech and deep house.

entries.title: Mix Of The Day: Malibu & Crystallmess
entries.description: Suspended between dream and dissociation.

entries.title: Mix Of The Day: Gizmoe
entries.description: A genre-traversing session that reaffirms the dance floor's status as a place of protest.

entries.title: Mix Of The Day: Dexter Colt & Sunday Lee
entries.description: Deep dance cuts for mind, body and soul.

entries.title: Mix Of The Day: d.jezzz
entries.description: Laidback beats for a sunset wind-down.

entries.title: Film: How Hyperdub Reimagined Modern Club Music
entries.description: Our new video essay traces the story of Hyperdub, a label that's operated at the bleeding edge of electronic music for over 20 years.

entries.title: 2 Live Crew lose appeal in bid to reclaim recording copyrights
entries.description: A US appeals court overturned a 2024 ruling, finding the group's copyright termination notice invalid due to a bankruptcy-related technicality.

entries.title: DJ Hell collaborates with AI artist on new EP U Can Dance With Me
entries.description: Namae Koi is "an AAVA at the crossroads of fashion, art and music," the German producer said.

entries.title: The Carry Nation announce new Nervous Records VA compilation
entries.description: Kim Anh, DJ Miss Parker and Jubilee feature on Full Tilt Carry Vol. 3.

entries.title: Mix Of The Day: Om Unit
entries.description: Tracing a forgotten thread between street soul and the hardcore continuum.

entries.title: Hong Kong's Shi Fu Miz Festival finalises tenth anniversary lineup
entries.description: Dr. Rubinstein, DJ MARIA. and Mr. Ho are among the new names added to the November one-dayer.

entries.title: Berlin club RSO announces six-date anniversary tour
entries.description: The Schöneweide space will host events in cities including Paris, Brussels and Tbilisi.

entries.title: New documentary to spotlight London's inclusive nightlife initiatives
entries.description: Radically Inclusive Clubbing follows members of Bubble Club, a long-running project for adults with learning disabilities and autism.

entries.title: New photobook captures the faces of the Berlin club scene
entries.description: Berlin Night After Glow features more than 200 portraits of the city's clubbing community.

entries.title: Pacha New York secures temporary liquor license
entries.description: The Ibiza nightlife brand is set to open on June 13th.
